data.title: Questor wins $9M contract to supply clean combustion solutions in Mexico, aiding in reducing emissions.
data.description: In Calgary, Alberta on September 25, 2025, Questor Technology Inc. announced securing a $9 million contract to provide clean combustion solutions to a major energy services company in Mexico for a three-year rental period. Trading on the TSX Venture Exchange as QST, Questor aims to help in the substantial reduction of flaring and methane emissions. The endeavor aligns with Mexico's goal to eliminate routine flaring entirely by 2030 and lower methane emissions by 30% from 2020 to 2030, in line with the Global Methane Pledge.

data.title: WuXi Biologics launches WuXia™4.0 with TrueSite TI™ platform averaging 8.0 g/L mAb titer - 99% clonal cell lines stable.
data.description: The latest generation of WuXia™ cell line platform, TrueSite TI™, utilizes a targeted integration method for efficient clone screening and maintains stable expression. This platform has demonstrated remarkable results, with an average monoclonal antibody (mAb) titer surpassing 8.0 g/L. Additionally, more than 99% of clonal cell lines have preserved their stability.

data.title: M2 Capital supports Ethena with $20M ENA token; TVL reaches $15B, gaining support from global investors.
data.description: Ethena's ENA token received a $20 million investment from M2 Capital as the protocol's Total Value Locked (TVL) approaches $15 billion. The project is gaining traction among global crypto investors, including notable institutional support.

data.title: Respira Therapeutics allows Gossamer Bio to acquire them in deal aimed at advancing RT234 for PAH and PH-ILD.
data.description: Respira Therapeutics, Inc., based in Palo Alto, California, announced on September 25, 2025, a strategic transaction aimed at advancing the development of RT234, a novel vardenafil Inhalation powder. This investigative drug is intended to be a first-in-class as-needed (PRN) therapy for Pulmonary Arterial Hypertension (PAH) and Pulmonary Hypertension associated with Interstitial Lung Disease (PH-ILD). The company has taken this step to expedite the development process of RT234.
